text:
Market today: Sentiment is cautious-to-nervous. The BIS (central bank of central banks) warning about AI-driven market froth is the real driver today — it directly pressures the high-multiple tech and chip names that make up almost your entire book. That's a headwind, not background noise.

Your holdings: Three need attention right now. AVGO is your biggest position and down 16% — that's a serious hole on margin, and it's on a SELL signal. PLTR is down 13% and also flagged SELL. SPAL is down nearly 17% and it's a 2x leveraged ETF that decays daily — every bad day costs you twice, and there's almost no cushion above your $19 stop. Margin amplifies all of these; a further leg down hits you harder than the percentages show.

Watch: First, whether that BIS warning gets picked up broadly and pressures chip/AI valuations further. Second, AVGO's price action around $336 stop — if it cracks, your largest position becomes a much bigger problem fast.

Bottom line: Your two SELLs (AVGO, PLTR) need a decision today, not tomorrow — margin means hesitation is a cost.
